content: {"position":"Classes are the superior choice for module organization.","confidence":0.80,"key_arguments":["Encapsulation of state","Inheritance for code reuse","Clear interface contracts"],"agrees_with_opponent":false,"counterpoints":[]}

{"position":"Classes have merit but functional patterns offer stronger composability.","confidence":0.65,"key_arguments":["Composition over inheritance reduces coupling","Pure functions are easier to test","Functional patterns align with current codebase"],"agrees_with_opponent":false,"counterpoints":["Closures provide encapsulation without class overhead","Modern bundlers tree-shake functions better than classes"]}

{"position":"After three rounds of discussion, the functional approach with selective class usage is optimal for this codebase.","confidence":0.85,"key_arguments":["Current codebase is 80% functional — consistency matters","Only 2 modules genuinely benefit from class encapsulation","Team familiarity with functional patterns reduces onboarding cost"],"agrees_with_opponent":true,"counterpoints":[]}
